VAL-CROSS-001.reason: Unauthenticated user visiting /my is correctly redirected to /login?callbackUrl=%2Fmy. However, after login, user lands on the callback URL (/my) instead of the Goods home page (/) as specified in the contract. The NextAuth callbackUrl mechanism redirects to the originally requested page, not to the Goods home page.

VAL-CROSS-003.reason: Unauthenticated user attempting to reach protected pages (/my, /my/orders, /products/1) is correctly redirected to /login with callbackUrl set. Auth gate blocks all protected routes.

VAL-CROSS-006.reason: From product detail page (/products/cmn5uzrot0002l060cnmfkh4p), browser back navigation successfully returns to the Goods listing page (/). Product grid is visible.

VAL-CROSS-007.reason: After clicking Log out on My page, session is cleared and user is redirected to /login. Subsequently navigating to /my redirects to /login?callbackUrl=%2Fmy, confirming auth gates are enforced on all protected routes post-logout.

VAL-CROSS-008.reason: After rapidly navigating through all 5 tabs (Goods -> Football -> Basketball -> Concert -> My), the My page still displays full user profile data (email, VIP level, balance, shares, integration points). Auth state is preserved throughout rapid tab switching.

VAL-CROSS-009.reason: Full end-to-end flow completed successfully: Login -> Goods home -> click product (Messi Jersey) -> product detail -> back to Goods -> Football tab -> click match (Jordan VS Argentina) -> event detail -> back to Football -> My page. All transitions smooth with correct content. Zero console errors throughout.

frictions.description: Product cards on the Goods page do not appear as interactive elements in agent-browser's interactive snapshot (-i mode). They are only visible in the full accessibility tree. Had to use 'find text' semantic locator to click them.
frictions.resolution: Used 'agent-browser find text "Lionel Messi" click' to interact with product cards instead of ref-based clicking.

summary: Tested 6 assertions: 5 passed, 1 failed. VAL-CROSS-001 fails because after login from a redirected protected route, the user lands on the callback URL instead of the Goods home page as specified. All other cross-area flows work correctly: auth gates block unauthenticated access, back navigation works, logout enforces auth, tab cycling preserves auth state, and the full browse flow completes smoothly with no console errors.
